qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Directory and model enumeration omit standalone .npy payloads
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory is an old-PyTorch scan_pytorch magic-number bypass, while this finding is a separate scan_directory_path/scan_huggingface_model extension allow-list omission that never reaches scan_numpy().

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: ZIP member scan bypass for protocol-0 pickles with benign names
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory targets old-PyTorch scan_pytorch magic-number parsing, but this finding is a separate scan_zip_bytes member-classification bypass for protocol-0 pickle entries stored under benign names.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. Both accepted findings are distinct scanner bypasses in file enumeration or ZIP member classification logic rather than the advisory's old-PyTorch magic-number parsing path.
evidence: GHSA-97f8-7cmv-76j2 describes an old-PyTorch bypass where picklescan's get_magic_number() only extracts literal INT/LONG opcodes and misses dynamically evaluated MAGIC_NUMBER values consumed by torch.load().

The fixing commit b9997634683a4f4bd0c7e3701e7ce7e90fe70e8c changes scan_pytorch() magic-number mismatch handling in src/picklescan/scanner.py; it does not modify directory enumeration or ZIP member detection logic.
directory_npy_omission_bypass is rooted in scan_directory_path() and scan_huggingface_model() omitting .npy files from their extension allow-lists before dispatch.

zip_protocol0_pickle_member_bypass is rooted in scan_zip_bytes() only scanning members with pickle-like extensions or binary-protocol magic, which skips protocol-0 pickle text stored under benign names.
